headline: Model Context Protocol breaks out as agent tooling and web standards converge on structured, typed actions.
body: Model Context Protocol enters as a new entrant alongside JSON-RPC, API integration, and web standards, anchored by tutorials on MCP client and server architecture and Google Cloud's agent configuration with the Agent Development Kit and Looker. Google Chrome's proposed WebMCP standard pushes the same idea to the browser, letting agents call named typed actions through structured tools rather than DOM automation. Tool configuration, Ollama, and Docker lead the movers as local agent stacks consolidate, shown in a masterclass running workspace and build agents privately on Gemma 4 via Ollama with Docker. Cursor and Baseten separately describe scaling to 128 parallel coding agents with inter-agent communication and review.
